title: silene involucrata subsp. angustiflora
intro: Silene involucrata subsp. angustiflora, also known as the narrow-flowered catchfly, is a flowering plant species within the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Silene involucrata subsp. angustiflora include its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
